claim_audit_results.claim_summary: MAESTRO is structured into nine work packages spanning 48 months
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/workpackage_seed.json — array contains exactly 9 entries (WP1–WP9); WP1.end_month=48 and WP9.end_month=48 confirm 48-month project duration

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: WP1 is Project Management and Coordination, led by Alpenstadt Technical University (ATU), spanning M1–M48
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/workpackage_seed.json#WP1 (title='Project Management and Coordination', lead='ATU', start_month=1, end_month=48); docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json#ATU (legal_name='Alpenstadt Technical University')

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: WP2 (Neuro-Symbolic Planning and Reasoning Engine, ATU) implements a hybrid neuro-symbolic planning engine combining MCTS and HTN planning with LLM-based heuristics and verifiable plan self-correction
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/workpackage_seed.json#WP2.description: 'integration of MCTS and HTN planning with LLM-based heuristics, plan monitoring and self-correction from verified intermediate states'; docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/roles.json#ATU.wp_lead=['WP1','WP2']

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: WP3 (Adaptive Memory Architecture, BIIS) delivers a unified tri-store memory architecture with a learned memory controller and forgetting mechanisms for long-horizon interactions
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/workpackage_seed.json#WP3.description: 'unified tri-store memory architecture (working, episodic, semantic memory) with a learned memory controller. Research on memory indexing, retrieval strategies, forgetting mechanisms'; docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/objectives.json#OBJ-2: 'learned memory controller that manages information flow between stores'

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: WP4 (Decentralised Multi-Agent Coordination, CERIA) develops formally specified task-delegation and negotiation protocols with convergence guarantees and auditable interaction traces
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/workpackage_seed.json#WP4.description: 'formally specified task-delegation and negotiation protocols for heterogeneous AI agents. Research on convergence guarantees...auditable multi-agent interaction traces'; docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/objectives.json#OBJ-3: 'provable convergence guarantees and auditable interaction traces'

claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/workpackage_seed.json#WP8 (lead='FIIT', start_month=6, end_month=48; description: 'Integrate the three research pillars and the External Tool and API Orchestration Layer into a unified MAESTRO framework'); docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/roles.json#FIIT.wp_lead=['WP8']

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: WP9 (Dissemination, Exploitation and Communication) led by BAL, manages open-source release and AI-on-demand platform contributions per SR-10 and CC-05
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/workpackage_seed.json#WP9 (lead='BAL', description: 'Manage open-source framework release...Coordinate AI-on-demand platform contributions'); docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/scope_requirements.json#SR-10: 'Communicable results must be shared with the European R&D community through the AI-on-demand platform'; docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/call_constraints.json#CC-05: same requirement confirmed

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Three parallel research WPs (WP2, WP3, WP4) all run M1–M36
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/workpackage_seed.json#WP2 (start=1, end=36), #WP3 (start=1, end=30), #WP4 (start=3, end=36)
claim_audit_results.flag_reason: Factual discrepancy with Tier 3 source: workpackage_seed.json shows WP3.end_month=30 (not 36 as stated), and WP4.start_month=3 (not M1 as implied). The claim that all three research WPs 'run M1–M36' is partially incorrect — WP3 ends at M30 and WP4 starts at M3. The section's own subsequent reference to 'D3-02 M30' is internally consistent with WP3 ending at M30, making the summary claim contradictory within the section itself.

All specific deliverable identifiers and delivery months are project-specific facts. docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/workpackage_seed.json confirms WP existence, leads, and durations but does not enumerate deliverables with IDs or months. The traceability_footer references docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json as the source for deliverable specifics, but this Tier 4 file is outside the TAPM declared input boundary for this skill invocation and cannot be independently verified. No Tier 1–3 accessible source enumerates these deliverable IDs or delivery months.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Critical path runs T2-01→T2-02→T2-03→T5-01→T5-02→T5-03→T8-03
claim_audit_results.flag_reason: Task-level identifiers (T2-01, T2-02, etc.) are project-specific facts not present in any Tier 1–3 accessible source. docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/workpackage_seed.json defines WPs but does not enumerate tasks within WPs. The traceability_footer references docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase4_gantt_milestones/gantt.json#critical_path, which is outside the TAPM declared input boundary and cannot be verified.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: The dependency graph contains 16 inter-WP data-input edges across nine tied critical paths of three edges each
claim_audit_results.flag_reason: Specific edge and critical-path counts are project-specific quantitative facts. docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/workpackage_seed.json describes WP participants but does not enumerate inter-WP dependency edges. The traceability_footer references docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json#dependency_map.edges, which is outside the TAPM declared input boundary and cannot be verified.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: The risk register covers 11 identified risks (RISK-01 through RISK-11)
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/risks.json — array contains exactly 11 entries with IDs RISK-01 through RISK-11

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: RISK-01 (neuro-symbolic integration performance) is medium likelihood/high impact, owned by ATU; mitigation includes modular architecture with fallback modes and integration testing from M6
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/risks.json#RISK-01 (probability='medium', impact='high', risk_owner='ATU', mitigation: 'Adopt a modular architecture...Implement fallback modes...Begin integration testing from M6, not M18')

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: RISK-04 (clinical data access delays) is medium/high, owned by NIHS; mitigation includes ethics applications submitted M1 and fallback to public datasets MIMIC-IV/eICU
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/risks.json#RISK-04 (probability='medium', impact='high', risk_owner='NIHS', mitigation: 'Submit ethics applications in M1...Use publicly available clinical datasets (MIMIC-IV, eICU)')

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: RISK-10 (framework integration complexity) is medium/high, owned by FIIT; mitigation includes shared API contracts defined M1–M3 with six-monthly integration checkpoints
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/risks.json#RISK-10 (probability='medium', impact='high', risk_owner='FIIT', mitigation: 'Define shared data representations, API contracts...in M1–M3...Conduct integration checkpoints every 6 months')

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Governance structure: General Assembly (biannual), Project Management Committee (quarterly), Ethics and Quality Oversight Committee (ATU and NIHS, as needed)
claim_audit_results.flag_reason: Governance body names, meeting cadences, and membership assignments are project-specific management facts. No Tier 1–3 accessible source (partners.json, roles.json, workpackage_seed.json, compliance_profile.json, or objectives.json) defines these governance bodies or their meeting frequency. The traceability_footer references docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase6_implementation_architecture/implementation_architecture.json#governance_matrix, which is outside the TAPM declared input boundary and cannot be verified.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Ethics flags triggered in Categories 8 (AI systems), 4 (health data), and 2 (human participants in clinical validation)
claim_audit_results.source_ref: Derivation: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/call_binding/compliance_profile.json confirms ethics_review_required=true. docs/tier2a_instrument_schemas/extracted/section_schema_registry.json#A.4 defines Category 2 as covering human participants in medical studies, Category 4 as covering personal data including health data, and Category 8 as covering AI system development and deployment. docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/objectives.json#OBJ-4 establishes clinical validation with de-identified clinical cases involving human participants, directly implicating Categories 2 and 4. The project develops and deploys AI systems across demonstrators, implicating Category 8. Inference: the three specific categories stated are the logically necessary consequence of the project scope as confirmed in Tier 3 and the category definitions in Tier 2A.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Eight-partner consortium spans six European countries
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json
claim_audit_results.flag_reason: Factual contradiction with Tier 3 source: partners.json lists 8 partners from 8 distinct countries — DE (ATU), FR (CERIA), NL (BIIS), IT (ISIA), SE (NIHS), CH (ELI), FI (BAL), ES (FIIT). The claim of 'six European countries' contradicts the source, which shows eight different country codes. The correct count is eight countries. This is a material factual error in the section.

claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json#ISIA (legal_name='Istituto Superiore di Ingegneria Avanzata', country='IT', description: 'AI-driven process optimisation, digital twin integration...AI for Manufacturing lab...operational industrial settings'); docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/roles.json#ISIA (wp_lead=['WP6']); docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/scope_requirements.json#SR-07: 'Proposals must demonstrate application in at least one Apply AI sector; the three specifically identified sectors are: healthcare, advanced manufacturing, and in-vehicle autonomous driving'
